_meta.title: Generic Capabilities — Maximum Deep Analysis
_meta.purpose: Full build guide for the 24 domain-agnostic platform capabilities extracted from sms-service. Each capability has DB schema, interfaces, behaviors, flows, rules, implementation patterns, and what NOT to copy. Build these BEFORE deciding which product features (SMS, broadcast, etc.) to add.
companion_files: docs/FOUNDATION_BUILD_PLAN.json — which slices to build in what order
_meta.reading_guide: Each capability has build_order (lower = earlier). Capabilities with same build_order can be built in parallel. Never start a capability before its depends_on list is complete.

capabilities.name: authentication
capabilities.summary: Email+password login with lockout, JWT access token issuance, and password reset flow.

columns: DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
DateLastUpdatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
TimeZoneId NVARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Romance Standard Time'
db_tables.notes: Soft delete only — never hard delete users. CurrentTenantId/CurrentWorkspaceId = 0 means not yet selected.

columns: DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: Multiple refresh tokens per user supported (multi-device). Cleanup expired tokens via background job.

IAuthenticationService: bool VerifyPassword(string password, string salt, string hash)
string GenerateJwt(User user, int tenantId, int workspaceId)

behaviors: Login: verify email exists → verify password hash → check lockout → auto-select workspace if exactly 1 → issue JWT+RefreshToken
Failed login: increment FailedLoginCount, set DateLastFailedLoginUtc. Time-scaled lockout after threshold (default 5).
Lockout reset: admin action resets FailedLoginCount=0, IsLockedOut=false
Password reset: generate GUID token → store on User → send email → validate token → set new hash → clear token
Soft delete: User.Deleted=true + DateDeletedUtc. Excluded from all queries unless explicitlyincluded.
Reactivate: clear Deleted flag

flows.name: Standard login
steps: Fetch User by email WHERE Deleted=0
Check lockout: IsLockedOut=true OR scaled lockout condition → HTTP 403
Auto-workspace: if CurrentWorkspaceId==0 AND user has exactly 1 workspace → assign
If multiple workspaces: set CurrentWorkspaceId=0 → client must call /api/auth/select-workspace → HTTP 300
Generate RefreshToken (GUID string), store in UserRefreshTokens, 480min sliding TTL

flows.name: Password reset
steps: Find user by email. If not found: return 200 anyway (prevent enumeration)
Generate GUID token, store User.PasswordResetToken + User.DatePasswordResetTokenExpiresUtc = now+15min
Send password reset email with link containing token
Validate: token exists, not expired, meets password policy

rules: Argon2id for password hashing — NOT SHA256 (ANTI-10 from sms-service lessons)
Lockout threshold: FailedLoginCount > 5 AND time-scaled delay. Admin reset required for permanent lockout.
Password policy minimum: 8 chars, digit + lowercase + uppercase + special char
PasswordResetToken expires after 15 minutes
Never return 'user not found' on password reset — always return 200 (prevent email enumeration)
Refresh tokens are sliding, not fixed — each successful use extends TTL
User.Email is unique. Case-insensitive match on lookup.
User is never hard-deleted
not_to_copy: SHA256(password+salt) pattern from sms-service — use Argon2id
Hardcoded TTL constants in controller — move to AppSettings (CAP-09)
CountryId == LanguageId design quirk in sms-service
capabilities.green_ai_note: sms-service uses SHA256 which is not a password KDF. Green-ai uses Argon2id from day 1. No migration needed — no legacy passwords.

capabilities.name: session_management
capabilities.summary: Per-request ICurrentUser resolution via JWT claims + DB User entity. Tenant and workspace context propagated into every request.

middleware.name: CurrentUserMiddleware
middleware.pipeline_position: After JWT bearer validation, before all controllers
steps: Load User from DB (cache by UserId per request)
Assert User.CurrentWorkspaceId != 0 → else HTTP 401 (or HTTP 300 if workspace selection needed)

behaviors: TenantId=0 → hard HTTP 401. No fallback, no bypass. Ever.
WorkspaceId=0 → hard HTTP 401 (or HTTP 300 if client needs to select)
LanguageId read from User.LanguageId in DB every request — NOT from JWT claims
ImpersonateFromUserId set when SuperAdmin impersonates — substitutestarget user as current
Refresh token issuance is the sync point between DB User.Current* and JWT claims

flows.name: Select workspace
steps: UPDATE Users SET CurrentWorkspaceId=@workspaceId WHERE Id=@userId
Issue new JWT with updated WorkspaceId

flows.name: Select tenant
steps: Issue new JWT. Client must then select workspace.

flows.name: Token refresh
steps: SELECT token FROM UserRefreshTokens WHERE Token=@token AND DateExpiresUtc > GETUTCDATE()
Issue new JWT with current User.Current* values

rules: Single ICurrentUser interface. Single middleware implementation. HARD RULE: no second implementation ever.
WorkspaceId in JWT is informational. DB User.CurrentWorkspaceId is authoritative.
Tenant switch clears CurrentWorkspaceId — requires workspace re-selection.
Token refresh reads DB state — never trust stale JWT claims for Current* values.
JobCurrentUser: batch context implements ICurrentUser with values from job metadata. Same interface.
not_to_copy: WebWorkContext duplication from sms-service (C_002) — two implementations diverged over years

capabilities.name: user_management
capabilities.summary: CRUD users, invite flow, status management. User is global — not tenant-scoped. Membership is via TenantUserMappings.

db_tables.notes: Already defined in CAP-01. Reused here.

behaviors: Create: generate password reset token → send invite email with link containing token
Invite: same as password reset flow — user lands on /set-password?token=<guid>
SoftDelete: set Deleted=true + DateDeletedUtc. User disappears from all normal queries.
Reactivate: clear Deleted=false + DateDeletedUtc=null
Update: name, email, languageId, timeZoneId. Email uniqueness validated.
Lockout reset: FailedLoginCount=0, IsLockedOut=false

flows.name: Admin invites new user
steps: Add to each WorkspaceUserMappings in workspaceIds
Generate GUID invite token → store as User.PasswordResetToken with 48h expiry
Send invite email: 'Click here to set your password: /set-password?token=<guid>'

rules: User exists once globally. Adding to multiple tenants = multiple TenantUserMappings rows.
Email is the unique key. Case-insensitive. Cannot reuse a deleted user's email without reactivation.
Admin cannot see users from other tenants unless SuperAdmin role.
Invite token expiry: 48 hours (configurable in AppSettings).
not_to_copy: User.CurrentProfileId stored on User entity — design quirk forces extra DB writes on every context switch

capabilities.name: multi_tenancy
capabilities.summary: Every data entity has TenantId as outer partition key. Explicit user-to-tenant membership. All SELECT queries MUST include WHERE TenantId = @currentTenantId.

columns: LanguageId INT NOT NULL FK→Languages(Id) DEFAULT 1
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: CountryId and LanguageId are separate in the green-ai model (unlike sms-service where they were the same value).

columns: DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
indexes: IX_TenantUserMappings_UserId NONCLUSTERED (UserId) — to find all tenants for a user
db_tables.notes: Explicit membership. Tenant membership does NOT auto-grant workspace access. No roles at this level.

rules: CRITICAL: Every feature table must have TenantId INT NOT NULL FK→Tenants(Id). No exceptions.
Every SELECT in service layer MUST include AND TenantId = @currentTenantId.
User.CurrentTenantId = 0 → HTTP 401 from CurrentUserMiddleware.
A user can be member of multiple tenants (M:M via TenantUserMappings).
Deleting a tenant = soft delete only. Data retention rules apply.
SuperAdmin can access all tenants. All other roles are bounded by their TenantUserMappings.
not_to_copy: CustomerUserRoleMappings(CustomerId, UserRoleId) naming confusion — no UserId = not a user assignment table, it's a policy table. Rename clearly.

capabilities.name: workspace_scoping
capabilities.summary: Sub-tenant operational context. All operational data (campaigns, recipient lists, templates) is scoped to Workspace, not just Tenant. Per-workspace capability flags control which features are active.

columns: ProfileTypeId INT NOT NULL FK→WorkspaceTypes(Id) DEFAULT 1
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: Every operational data table has WorkspaceId FK. This is the primary data partition for all work.

columns: DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: Tenant membership does NOT auto-grant workspace access. Must be explicit.
Seeded enum table. Names are the capability identifiers. Domain-specific capabilities (CanSendBySms, CanSendByVoice etc.) are added here as the product features are planned.
Seeded reference. sms-service values: Water, WasteWater, Electricity, Heating, Renovation, Standard. Green-ai: define from product requirements.

rules: Every operational table MUST have WorkspaceId INT NOT NULL FK→Workspaces(Id).
Tenant membership does NOT grant workspace access — WorkspaceUserMappings must be explicit.
WorkspaceId=0 in CurrentUser → HTTP 401.
Workspace soft-delete: Workspace.Deleted=true. Data is retained for compliance period.
A workspace can only belong to one tenant (TenantId is NOT NULL on Workspace).
WorkspaceCapabilities seed = empty at launch. Domain features add their capability names to this table at startup.
not_to_copy: 63 hardcoded ProfileRoleNames values — these are domain-specific. WorkspaceCapabilities starts empty and grows with features.
ProfileGroupRole (template at creation time only) — if workspace templates are needed, design them as first-class feature, not side-effect of capability assignment

capabilities.name: role_based_access_control
capabilities.summary: Two independent RBAC systems: global admin UserRoles (per user, no tenant scope) + per-workspace capability flags. Both are flat junction tables — no inheritance.

seed_values: SuperAdmin — full system access. TenantId restriction: TenantId in {1,2} (operator tenant only).
ManageUsers — can invite/remove users within own tenant
ManageWorkspaces — can create/edit workspaces within own tenant
API — can use public API endpoints
AlwaysTestMode — all sends go to test mode
RequiresApproval — broadcasts require second-user approval before sending
db_tables.notes: These are seeded roles. Add more as platform features require them. Names are the authority — not IDs.
NO TenantId column. UserRoles are GLOBAL across all tenant memberships. This is intentional — admin roles apply everywhere. (sms-service does the same; phase 2 can add tenant-scoped roles if needed.)

POLICY table — no UserId. Defines which UserRoles a tenant has configured/purchased. Used to gate role assignment UI. (Renamed from sms-service's CustomerUserRoleMappings which was confusing because it had no UserId.)

rules: UserRoleMappings has NO TenantId — UserRoles are global across all of a user's tenant memberships.
SuperAdmin access: TenantId of operator organization only. Can access all tenants.
WorkspaceCapabilityMappings controls OPERATIONAL features. UserRoleMappings controls ADMIN capabilities.
The two systems are independent — a user can have ManageUsers role AND a workspace can have CanSendBySms capability. They are orthogonal.
Role names are the authority, not IDs. Never hardcode role IDs — always look up by name.
not_to_copy: CustomerUserRoleMappings naming — no UserId, highly confusing name → renamed to TenantRolePolicies
UserRoleMappings with hardcoded numeric ID checks in business logic

capabilities.name: permission_service
capabilities.summary: Single injectable IPermissionService. All access and capability checks route through this service. Backed by Dapper + in-memory cache per request.
behaviors: CanUserAccessTenant: SELECT 1 FROM TenantUserMappings WHERE TenantId=@t AND UserId=@u
CanUserAccessWorkspace: SELECT 1 FROM WorkspaceUserMappings WHERE WorkspaceId=@w AND UserId=@u
DoesUserHaveRole: SELECT 1 FROM UserRoleMappings urm JOIN UserRoles ur ON urm.RoleId=ur.Id WHERE urm.UserId=@u AND ur.Name=@role
DoesWorkspaceHaveCapability: SELECT 1 FROM WorkspaceCapabilityMappings wcm JOIN WorkspaceCapabilities wc ON wcm.CapabilityId=wc.Id WHERE wcm.WorkspaceId=@w AND wc.Name=@cap
In-memory cache scoped per HTTP request (not global cache — permissions can change between requests)
rules: All business logic that checks permissions MUST use IPermissionService. No raw SQL permission checks in feature code.
Cache: per-request scope only. Do not use distributed cache for permissions — stale permissions are a security issue.
SuperAdmin bypasses CanUserAccessTenant and CanUserAccessWorkspace (but NOT DoesWorkspaceHaveCapability — capabilities are workspace-level policy, not user privilege).
Every HTTP controller action that is tenant/workspace-scoped must call at least one CanUserAccess check.
401 vs 403: unauthenticated = 401. Authenticated but no access = 403.
Capability names are string constants defined in a static CapabilityNames class — never magic strings in feature code.

capabilities.name: federated_authentication
capabilities.summary: SAML2 SSO per tenant, Azure AD / Entra ID (MSAL), SCIM 2.0 inbound user provisioning. All converge at JWT issuance.

columns: TenantId INT NOT NULL UNIQUE FK→Tenants(Id)
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
DateLastUpdatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: One SAML IdP config per tenant. Certificate stored in Azure Key Vault in production (not in DB).

added_columns: ScimTokenHash NVARCHAR(500) NULL — SHA256 of SCIM bearer token. In sms-service this was stored plaintext (ScimTokenUUID) — green-ai stores the hash.

flows.name: SAML2 SSO
steps: Redirect to IdP with SAML2 AuthnRequest
Validate signature, extract email from configured claim
Find or create User by email
Issue JWT — same path as standard login

flows.name: SCIM sync
steps: External HR system sends SCIM 2.0 POST/PUT/DELETE to /scim/v2/Users

rules: SAML certificate renewal is an operational procedure — must be documented and automated.
SCIM token stored as hash (SHA256) — never plaintext (sms-service stored ScimTokenUUID plaintext — security issue).
All federated auth paths converge at the same JWT issuance function as standard login.
DEFERRED from phase 1 — build only when first tenant requests SSO.

capabilities.name: app_settings
capabilities.summary: Typed key-value application settings stored in DB. Full-load cache with invalidation on save. All credentials and feature flags accessed via IApplicationSettingService.

columns: Id INT PK — matches SettingKey enum value
DateLastUpdatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: One row per SettingKey enum value. CreateDefault() seeds all rows idempotently at startup.

seed_settings.note: The SettingKey enum is defined in the platform layer. Domain features add their own keys. Minimum platform keys at launch:

IApplicationSettingService: bool GetBool(SettingKey key, bool defaultValue = false)

behaviors: Full cache: all settings loaded into memory at startup. Cache key: typically 'platform.applicationsettings'
Save: persists to DB AND invalidates cache (so next Get() reloads all from DB)
CreateDefaults: idempotent INSERT IF NOT EXISTS for all SettingKey enum members
Get returns defaultValue if key not found (never throws)
rules: All external credentials (SMTP, blob, carrier APIs) MUST flow through IApplicationSettingService.
Never read credentials from appsettings.json directly in service code.
SettingKey enum values are the IDs used as ApplicationSettings.Id. Add new keys to the end — never reorder.
Sensitive values (passwords, API keys) must be stored encrypted in production (KMS or Azure Key Vault envelope encryption).
IsMaintenanceMode kill-switch can be checked on every request in middleware — returns HTTP 503 to all non-SuperAdmin requests.

capabilities.name: localization
capabilities.summary: Multi-language label storage and resolution. ILocalizationService.GetString(key, languageId) is the single entry point. Unknown keys return the key name — never null.

db_tables.seed: Danish(1,da-DK,da), Norwegian(5,nb-NO,no), English(3,en-GB,en) as minimum. Add Swedish, Finnish, German as needed.

columns: ResourceName NVARCHAR(200) NOT NULL — dot-separated key, e.g. 'shared.save', 'errors.loginFailed'
ResourceValue NVARCHAR(MAX) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Missing Translation'
db_tables.notes: When inserting a new key, auto-create rows for ALL published languages. Non-specified languages get 'Missing Translation'.

ILocalizationService: Task InsertOrUpdateAsync(string resourceKey, int languageId, string value)

behaviors: GetString: query LocaleStringResources WHERE ResourceName=@key AND LanguageId=@languageId. If not found → return @key as fallback (never null, never exception).
GetAllForLanguage: returns full dictionary for client-side label loading. Endpoint: GET /api/localization/{languageId}.
InsertOrUpdate: if key doesn't exist → create in all published languages with 'Missing Translation'. Then set the provided language's value.
Cache: full in-memory dictionary per language. Invalidated when any label is updated.
rules: Unknown key returns the key name — NEVER null, NEVER empty string, NEVER exception.
ActivityLog entries store ResourceKey only — resolved at read time via GetString(entry.ResourceKey, user.LanguageId).
Email templates use GetString for subject and body. Never hardcode English strings in service code.
LanguageId is read from ICurrentUser.LanguageId per request — NOT from JWT, NOT from browser Accept-Language.
Minimum seed: 200 labels covering login, navigation, common errors, and form validation.
api_endpoints: GET /api/localization/{languageId} — returns full label dictionary for client side
GET /api/localization/languages — returns list of published languages

capabilities.name: structured_logging
capabilities.summary: ISystemLogger with UserId+TenantId context stamps. Multiple output targets. Outbound HTTP auto-logged via DelegatingHandler.

columns: LogLevelId INT NOT NULL — maps to LogLevel enum
Module NVARCHAR(200) NULL — source component name
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: Logs older than retention period deleted by cleanup batch job.

columns: RequestedOnUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: Only written when AppSetting RequestLogLevel >= 1. High-volume — add TTL and partitioning.

columns: DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: Written by OutgoingHttpClientLoggingHandler DelegatingHandler — auto-injected into ALL HttpClient instances registered in DI.

ISystemLogger: Task LogDebugAsync(string message, string? module = null)
Task LogInfoAsync(string message, string? module = null)
Task LogWarningAsync(string message, string? module = null, string? detail = null)
Task LogErrorAsync(string message, Exception? ex = null, string? module = null)
Task LogFatalAsync(string message, Exception? ex = null, string? module = null)

implementations.name: ApplicationInsightsLogger
implementations.note: Wrap TelemetryClient — do not call AppInsights SDK directly in feature code

implementations.name: FatalEmailLogger
implementations.note: Sends to operator alert email address. Must NOT use the async email queue — send synchronously to survive app crash scenarios.

implementations.name: OutgoingHttpClientLoggingHandler
implementations.note: Logs URL, method, request/response body, status code, duration. Sensitive data redaction required.

behaviors: RequestResponseLoggingMiddleware: captures HTTP request+response body. Only active when AppSetting(RequestLogLevel) > 0.
Fatal log triggers immediate email notification to ops team.
OutgoingHttpClientLoggingHandler is transparent — no change to HttpClient usage in feature code.
rules: Call ISystemLogger in feature code — never call ILogger<T>, AppInsights SDK, or Serilog directly.
All Logs entries include UserId + TenantId from ICurrentUser (null for anonymous/background requests).
Request body logging must strip sensitive fields (passwords, tokens, card numbers).
RequestLogs are high-volume — set AppSetting(RequestLogLevel) to off in production by default.
OutgoingRequestLogs are high-volume — may need partitioning or shorter retention in production.

capabilities.name: audit_trail
capabilities.summary: Compliance audit log using translation keys (not resolved text). Stores key+parameters at write time. Resolved at read time via ILocalizationService.

columns: ObjectId INT NOT NULL — ID of the entity this log is about
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
ResourceKey NVARCHAR(200) NOT NULL — translation key, e.g. 'activityLog.broadcast.created'
ResourceParameters NVARCHAR(MAX) NULL — JSON array of substitution values
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
indexes: IX_ActivityLogEntries_ResourceKey_DateCreated NONCLUSTERED (ResourceKey, DateCreatedUtc DESC) — for compliance audit queries

db_tables.notes: Extensible — domain features add their own types at startup.

IActivityLogService: Task CreateLogAsync(string typeName, int objectId, string resourceKey, string[]? parameters = null)
Task<IReadOnlyList<ActivityLogReadModel>> GetLogsAsync(string typeName, int objectId, int languageId, int maxRecords = 50)

behaviors: Write: insert ActivityLog header (if not exists for typeName+objectId) → insert ActivityLogEntry row (ResourceKey + JSON parameters)
Read: load ActivityLog + entries → resolve ResourceKey via ILocalizationService.GetString(key, userLanguageId) → substitute parameters → return human-readable list
Batch write: single DB round-trip for bulk import completions
rules: ActivityLogEntries store ResourceKey ONLY — never a resolved string. Language is user preference at read time.
ActivityLogEntries are immutable once written. No update/delete.
IX_ActivityLogEntries_ResourceKey_DateCreated enables compliance audit: 'who deleted which user in this date range'.
ResourceKey names follow convention: 'activityLog.{entity}.{action}' — e.g. 'activityLog.user.deleted', 'activityLog.broadcast.approved'.

capabilities.name: transactional_email
capabilities.summary: System-initiated emails: password reset, user invite, critical alerts. Separate code path from broadcast/delivery email. Must work even when delivery pipeline is down.

IEmailService: Task SendAsync(string toEmail, string subject, string htmlBody, string? textBody = null)
Task SendPasswordResetAsync(string toEmail, string resetToken, int languageId)
Task SendInviteAsync(string toEmail, string inviteToken, string tenantName, int languageId)

rules: Transactional email sends SYNCHRONOUSLY for fatal alerts — do not queue. Fatal alert must survive app restart.
All other transactional emails can be async (fire-and-forget with logging on failure).
Subject and body content come from LocaleStringResources — never hardcoded English strings.
Separate from broadcast email path: IEmailService vs IDeliveryService. Never share code.
Retry: 3 attempts with 1s delay. Log failure if all attempts fail. Never swallow silently.

capabilities.name: background_jobs
capabilities.summary: IHostedService workers for queue processing. Quartz.NET for cron scheduling. Azure Batch for large-scale jobs. JobCurrentUser for batch context.

db_tables.notes: Seeded reference. One row per known job type.

columns: DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: Polled by ClientEventBackgroundService → pushed to browser via SSE. Short TTL (5-10 min).

JobCurrentUser.description: Implements ICurrentUser for non-HTTP execution contexts (batch jobs, background services). Populated from job metadata, not from JWT.
JobCurrentUser.implementation: class JobCurrentUser : ICurrentUser { public int UserId => jobContext.UserId; public int TenantId => jobContext.TenantId; ... }
JobCurrentUser.registration: Register in DI with Scoped lifetime within job execution scope.
JobCurrentUser.critical_rule: Every feature that uses ICurrentUser must work identically whether called from HTTP or batch context. Design features assuming either.

IHostedService_pattern.loop: while (!cancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested) { await DoWorkAsync(); await Task.Delay(pollingInterval, cancellationToken); }
IHostedService_pattern.error_handling: Catch all exceptions per iteration. Log and continue — never let an unhandled exception stop the service.
IHostedService_pattern.graceful_shutdown: Respect cancellationToken on shutdown. Finish current item, do not start next.

rules: JobCurrentUser: same ICurrentUser interface, different population path. No special-casing in feature code.
IHostedService workers: per-server queue isolation. Web server 1 cannot push to web server 2's queue.
Time-sensitive items (pin codes, single sends) go in IHostedService queue. Bulk jobs go in Azure Batch.
Every job execution creates a JobTask + JobTaskStatus row for monitoring.
Background services must log start, completion, and errors via ISystemLogger.

capabilities.name: health_monitoring
capabilities.summary: Health endpoint for orchestrators + Azure Monitor / Application Insights. Non-authenticated.
api_endpoints: GET /health — returns {status, version, db, cache, timestamp}. HTTP 200 = healthy, 503 = degraded/unhealthy
GET /health/detail (SuperAdmin only) — returns per-dependency status with latency
checks: ApplicationSettings cache: cache loaded = healthy. Not loaded = degraded.
Blob storage connectivity (optional): GET container properties.
rules: GET /health requires NO authentication. Load balancers and orchestrators call it.
Never return sensitive information (connection strings, API keys) in health response.
HTTP 200 = healthy or degraded (but still serving). HTTP 503 = unhealthy (remove from load balancer).
Azure Monitor alert: trigger on 2+ consecutive 503 responses within 5 minutes.

capabilities.name: admin_ui_operations
depends_on: CAP-01 through CAP-07 (all identity + tenancy + auth)
capabilities.summary: Multi-tier admin: SuperAdmin, TenantAdmin, WorkspaceAdmin. Tenant CRUD, user invite, workspace assignment, capability management, onboarding template clone.

onboarding_clone.name: CloneWorkspaceTemplate
onboarding_clone.description: Copy a template workspace (capability set + initial config) to a new customer at onboarding. Equivalent to sms-service's CloneProspectProductsToCustomer.
onboarding_clone.pattern: Admin selects template workspace → system copies WorkspaceCapabilityMappings → optionally copies initial seed data

rules: Admin operations are blocked in FOUNDATION_BUILD_PLAN.json until SLICE-006 passes — circular dependency on identity_access write path.
Impersonation: SuperAdmin only. Logs ImpersonatingUserId in ActivityLog. JWT carries ImpersonateFromUserId.
All admin mutations create ActivityLog entries.

capabilities.name: file_storage_abstraction
capabilities.summary: IBlobStorageService over Azure Blob Storage. Typed container name constants. Upload, download, delete, SAS URL generation.

columns: Name NVARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE — e.g. 'csv', 'xlsx', 'png', 'pdf'
db_tables.notes: Allowlist of accepted file types. FileTypeIdFromExtension(ext) returns null = reject upload.

IBlobStorageService: Task<string> UploadAsync(string containerName, string blobName, Stream content, string contentType)
Task<string> GetSasUrlAsync(string containerName, string blobName, TimeSpan ttl)

container_constants.rule: Container names are constants — never magic strings in feature code.

rules: Never call Azure.Storage.Blobs SDK directly in feature code — always use IBlobStorageService.
Upload validation: check file extension against FileTypes allowlist BEFORE calling IBlobStorageService.UploadAsync.
SAS URL TTL should be short (15-60 min) for file downloads.
Error log blobs: write critical failure details to BlobContainers.ErrorLogs when DB write fails.

capabilities.name: real_time_push
capabilities.summary: Server-Sent Events (SSE) for pushing background job status to browser. ClientEvents table polled by IHostedService. Simpler than WebSocket for one-way push.

flow: Background service updates job status → inserts ClientEvents row
ClientEventBackgroundService polls ClientEvents table every 1-2 sec
For each new event: send to subscribed SSE clients matching EventKey filter
Client receives event → updates UI without polling REST endpoint

rules: SSE is one-directional: server → client. Use WebSocket only if bidirectional is needed.
ClientEvents rows have short TTL (10-15 min). Cleaned up by background job.
Client reconnects automatically on disconnect (SSE spec behavior).
Authentication: SSE endpoint requires valid JWT. Send token as query param or Authorization header.

capabilities.name: contact_lists
capabilities.summary: Named, workspace-scoped lists of external contacts. Groups for collective addressing. Expanded to individual contacts at use-time. Optional SCIM sync.

columns: DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()

rules: ContactLists are per workspace. One workspace does not see another workspace's lists.
Phone validation at add-time: E.164 format required if phone is set.
Contact list expansion (resolve group to individuals) happens AT USE TIME — not when the list is saved.
Capability gate: WorkspaceCapability 'ContactLists' must be enabled.
SCIM sync: if TenantSamlSettings.ScimTokenHash is set → accept SCIM User push → sync to Contacts.

capabilities.name: message_templates
capabilities.summary: Workspace-scoped parameterized message templates with merge fields {{key}}. Per-channel variants (text, HTML). Template engine must be chosen before implementation.

columns: Subject NVARCHAR(500) NULL — for email channel
BodyHtml NVARCHAR(MAX) NULL — for email channel
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
DateLastUpdatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()

options.name: Simple {{key}} substitution
options.pros: Zero dependencies, easy to understand, safe

template_engine_decision.recommendation: Simple {{key}} substitution for v1. Scriban if conditional logic in templates is required.
template_engine_decision.decision_rule: Choose ONE engine before writing first template. Document in ai-governance decision registry.

rules: Template bodies are workspace-owned — one workspace does not see another's templates.
Merge field keys are defined in a MergeFieldNames static class — never magic strings.
Template rendering happens at SEND TIME — not at creation time.
Attachments are stored in Azure Blob (CAP-17). Template stores only the blob path reference.

capabilities.name: bulk_import
capabilities.summary: File upload (CSV/XLSX) → validate → configurable column mappings → background processing. SavedImportConfiguration for reuse.

columns: ColumnMappings NVARCHAR(MAX) NOT NULL — JSON: [{sourceColumn, targetField}]
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
ErrorDetails NVARCHAR(MAX) NULL — JSON array of {row, error}
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()

flows.name: Bulk import
steps: Validate file: extension in FileTypes allowlist, size < MaxImportFileSizeMb
Background job: download file from blob, parse rows, validate, insert domain records
On completion: update ImportJob.Status=Completed, write ActivityLog entry
Client polls GET /api/import/{importJobId} or receives SSE event on completion

rules: Import always runs in background — never block the HTTP request for large files.
Row errors should NOT fail the entire import — record error rows and continue.
ActivityLog entry on completion: 'Import of {N} contacts completed. {E} errors.'
ImportConfigurations allow non-technical users to save their column mapping for reuse.

capabilities.name: webhook_delivery
capabilities.summary: Workspace registers outbound webhook URL. System delivers events via HTTP POST with exponential retry and delivery log.

columns: EventTypes NVARCHAR(MAX) NOT NULL — JSON array of event names, e.g. ['broadcast.completed', 'import.completed']
SecretHash NVARCHAR(500) NOT NULL — HMAC-SHA256 signature key hash
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
DateAttemptedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()

rules: Webhook payload must be signed: HMAC-SHA256(payload, secret). Client verifies signature.
Retry: exponential backoff. Attempts: 1, 5, 30, 120, 480 minutes. After 5 failures: mark subscription inactive.
Delivery attempt log: keeps all attempts for debugging.
Secret stored as hash — never expose raw secret after registration.
Capability gate: WorkspaceCapability 'Webhooks' must be enabled.

capabilities.name: public_api
capabilities.summary: Workspace-scoped API keys (hash stored, plaintext returned once). Versioned REST API. Capability gate: 'PublicApi' workspace capability.

columns: KeyHash NVARCHAR(500) NOT NULL — SHA256(key). NEVER store plaintext.
KeyPrefix NVARCHAR(10) NOT NULL — first 6 chars of key for display (e.g. 'sk_liv_')
Name NVARCHAR(200) NOT NULL — admin-assigned label
DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()

api_versioning.note: Define from day 1. Adding /v2/ later is cheap; retrofitting versioning to unversioned URLs is expensive.

rules: API key: generate as 'sk_live_' + 32-random-chars. Store SHA256(key). NEVER store plaintext.
Return plaintext key EXACTLY ONCE at creation. Display KeyPrefix only for identification thereafter.
API key auth: Authorization: Bearer <key>. Extract WorkspaceId from DB lookup.
Capability gate: WorkspaceCapability 'PublicApi' required.
Rate limiting: configure per workspace, start at 100 req/min.
not_to_copy: sms-service ProfileApiKeys stores key plaintext — security vulnerability (ANTI-04)

capabilities.name: consent_management
capabilities.summary: Opt-in list management for outbound messaging. Filter chain: consent check → duplicate removal → restriction check. Filtered records preserved with reason for compliance.

columns: DateCreatedUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
Identifier NVARCHAR(200) NOT NULL — phone, email, or custom reference
ConsentSource NVARCHAR(200) NULL — how they consented (web form, import, etc.)
FilterReason NVARCHAR(100) NOT NULL — typed enum: 'NotInConsentList', 'Duplicate', 'OutsideScope', 'NationalOptOut'
DateFilteredUtc DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
db_tables.notes: Compliance artifact. Must not be deleted within GDPR retention period.

rules: Filter chain is ordered: 1) National opt-out (KRR etc.), 2) Consent list gate (if enabled), 3) Duplicate, 4) Scope restriction.
FilterReason is a typed string constant — never free-text. Compliance audit must be able to query by reason.
FilteredRecipients are immutable once written.
Capability gate: WorkspaceCapability 'ConsentManagement' enables consent list enforcement.

capabilities.name: data_retention
capabilities.summary: Per-tenant configurable data retention periods. Background batch job deletes old records. C# only — no stored procedures. GDPR compliance.

added_columns: MonthsToRetainDeliveryData INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 60 — overrides system default per tenant

behaviors: CleanupJob runs on schedule (daily/weekly). For each tenant: DELETE FROM DeliveryRecords WHERE DateCreatedUtc < DATEADD(MONTH, -Tenant.MonthsToRetainDeliveryData, GETUTCDATE()).
Log retention: DELETE FROM Logs WHERE DateCreatedUtc < DATEADD(MONTH, -Tenant.MonthsToRetainLogs, GETUTCDATE()).
Request logs: shorter retention (e.g. 30 days).
FilteredRecipients: retain for compliance period (cannot delete until GDPR erasure request).
rules: C# only — NO stored procedures. Must be debuggable and auditable.
Cleanup job logs: ActivityLog entry per tenant per run: 'Deleted N delivery records for tenant X'.
Soft-deleted entities: same retention rules apply to Deleted=true rows.
GDPR erasure request overrides retention — user erasure must include all tables with UserId.
Test in staging: run cleanup with DRY_RUN=true flag that logs what WOULD be deleted without deleting.

build_order_summary.description: Which capabilities can be built in parallel (same order) vs which must wait.
build_order_summary.golden_core_note: These 11 capabilities constitute the minimal viable portal. A developer can log in, see their workspace, and the system is ready for the first domain feature.
